Study Hub
Study Hub / Pak Study / Testpoint / Lesson 9

Lesson 9

Questions 1601–1800 · Tap a card to reveal the explanation.

1

What is the northernmost administered point of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Tri-junction point
Show explanation
Explanation

The tri-junction point is where the borders of Pakistan, China, and India meet.

It is the northernmost point administered by Pakistan.

2

Which Range is called Roof of the World?

Correct Answer: Pamir Plateau
Show explanation
Explanation

The Tibetan Plateau (Pamir Plateau) is known as the Roof of the World.

It's also the thickest crust in the world, between 70 and 90 kilometers.

The plateau is the world's largest and highest.

Its average elevation is more than 4,500 meters (14,764 feet).


Additional information 

The Shandur Pass in Pakistan is also sometimes called "the roof of the world".

3

Who was the political heir of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan?

Correct Answer: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk
Show explanation
Explanation

Political and Institutional Heir of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan – Key Points

Heir: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk

After Sir Syed’s death (1898): He became Secretary of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College.

Role: Led the Muhammadan Educational Conference and continued Sir Syed’s educational and political work.

Significance: Served as the immediate political and institutional successor of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

4

Which of the following political parties did not boycott the Simon Commission?

Correct Answer: All India Muslim League
Show explanation
Explanation

Simon's commission was rejected by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League


The All India Muslim League divided into two groups.

  • Jinnah Group
  • Shafi Group

Quaid Group boycotted the Simon Commission but Shafi Group was against boycotting this commission.


Simon Commission was appointed in 1928


It comprises 7 members of the British


It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi

5

Which province opposed the One Unit Scheme in 1955?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan .

Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954.

and passed on 30 September 1955

This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

  • West Punjab
  • Sind
  • NWFP
  • Baluchistan

Additional information:

The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970

General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.

6

Which glacier feeds the Hunza River?

Correct Answer: Batura Glacier
Show explanation
Explanation

The Batura Glacier is the main source of the Hunza River in Gilgit-Baltistan.

It is one of the largest glaciers outside the polar regions.

7

Which of the following pass connects Sindh Plain with Quetta?

Correct Answer: Bolan Pass
Show explanation
Explanation

Important Passes of Pakistan

1. Khyber Pass 

connects: Peshawar to Kabul

connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


2. Bolan Pass 

Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


3. Lowari Pass

Connects: Chitral with Dir 

Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


4. Broghil Pass 

Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


5. Malakand Pass 

Connects: Dir with Chitral 

Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


6. Shandur pass

Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

Other name: Friendship Gate

Connects: China with Pakistan 

Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


8. Dorah pass

Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


9. Babusar Pass

Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


10. Kohat Pass

Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


11. Khojak Pass

Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


12. Tochi Pass

Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

8

Which sector of Pakistan pays the most taxes?

Correct Answer: Salaried class
Show explanation
Explanation

The salaried class contributes most to income tax through payroll deductions.

Other sectors like agriculture and small businesses often have lower tax compliance.

9

The Security Council passed its first Resolution on Kashmir on ____?

Correct Answer: January 17, 1948
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The security council passed its first resolution on Kashmir on January 17, 1948.
  • ***
  •  United Nations Security Council Resolution 39, adopted on January 20, 1948.
  • The United Nations Security Council Resolution 47, adopted on 21 April 1948, concerns the resolution of the Kashmir conflict.
10

The First Muslim ruler to introduce the System of Price Control in the sub-continent was ______?

Correct Answer: Allau ud din Khilji
Show explanation
Explanation

The system of market control or price control was introduced by Alauddin Khalji during the Sultanate period.

He was the second ruler of the Khilji Dynasty in India



AGM 24-12-2022


11

The Upper Indus Plain ends at _____?

Correct Answer: Mithankot
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus River starts in Tibet

The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

National River of Pakistan: Indus River

6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

Indus River Length: 3,180 km

Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

12

Which City of Pakistan is famous for sports goods?

Correct Answer: Sialkot
Show explanation
Explanation
Sialkot is a city located in Punjab, Pakistan.
It is the capital of the Sialkot District
Sialkot is the 13th most populous city in Pakistan
Sialkot is located in the north-east Punjab
Sialkot is one of Pakistan's most industrialized regions.
The postal code of Sialkot is  51310
The calling code of Sialkot is 052
Sialkot is known for manufacturing and export of many items:

such as
  1. surgical instruments
  2. musical instruments
  3. sports goods
  4. leather goods
  5. textile goods 

13

When National Bank of Pakistan established?

Correct Answer: 1949
Show explanation
Explanation

National Bank was created on November 9, 1949, under National Bank of Pakistan Ordinance, 1949 for the purpose.

The NBP has over 1,500 branches in Pakistan and more than 18 locations overseas.

14

When State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) was inaugurated?

Correct Answer: 1st July 1948
Show explanation
Explanation

The State Bank of Pakistan was inaugurated on 1st July 1948.

The headquarters of the State Bank of Pakistan in Karachi Sindh.

History of State Bank

Zahid Husain was the founder and the first governor of the SBP from June 1948 to July 1953.

Dr. Shamshad Akhtar was the first female Governor of the SBP in 2006.

Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the State Bank of Pakistan.

The Governor is appointed by the President of Pakistan.

Term of five years.

15

Pakistan’s first economic budget was presented in which year?

Correct Answer: 1948
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan’s First Budget – Key Points

Presented in: February 1948

Finance Minister: Malik Ghulam Muhammad

Significance: Marked the start of Pakistan’s financial planning after independence

Outcome: Led to the conceptualization of the first Five-Year Plan around 1950 (implemented later)

16

Where Jhelum River starts?

Correct Answer: Held Kashmir
Show explanation
Explanation

The Jhelum River originates from a deep spring at Vernag in western Jammu and Kashmir.

The river flows northwest from the Pir Panjal Range through the Vale of Kashmir.

It empties into Wular Lake at Srinagar, which regulates its flow.

17

Which province of Pakistan has the largest number of divisions?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

There are total 37 Divisions in Pakistan.

Punjab: 10 Divisions

Balochistan: 08 Divisions

KPK: 07 Divisions

Sindh: 06 Divisions

Gilgit-Baltistan: 03 Divisions

Azad Kashmir: 03 Divisions


There are total 170 Districts in Pakistan.

Punjab: 42 Districts

KPK: 38 Districts

Balochistan: 36 Districts

Sindh: 30 Districts

Gilgit-Baltistan: 14 Districts

Azad Kashmir: 10 Districts

18

When did Pakistan officially recognize/accept the Government of Bangladesh?

Correct Answer: February 1974
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan officially recognized the Government of Bangladesh on February 22, 1974
  • During the 2nd OIC meeting in Lahore.
  • Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto announced the recognition
  • Pressure from Arab leaders to invite Bangladesh's Sheikh Mujibur Rehman to the conference

Additional information 

List of countries in order of their recognition of Bangladesh on 22 February 1974

  • Malagasy: 14 April 1972
  • Iraq 18 July 1972
  • Upper Volta: 19 September 1972
  • Pakistan: 22 February 1974

****

Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

19

Kalash Valley is situated in ______?

Correct Answer: Chitral
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kalash Valleys are located in Chitral District in northern (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa) Pakistan.

More specifically, they are surrounded by the Hindu Kush mountain range.

It is home to the Kalash people, known for their unique culture, language, and festivals.

20

The ruins of Harappa are situated in which district?

Correct Answer: Sahiwal
Show explanation
Explanation

Harappa, village in eastern Punjab province, eastern Pakistan

The ruins of Harappa are situated in   Sahiwal District, Punjab, Pakistan

The Bronze Age Harappan civilization, now more often called the Indus Valley Civilisation

Harappa is small crossroads town of 15,000 people today.
21

Ch. Rehmat Ali graduated from which institution?

Correct Answer: Islamia College Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Ch. Rehmat Ali graduated from Islamia College Lahore.

It was affiliated with the University of Punjab at the time.

After obtaining his initial degree, he pursued further studies at the University of Cambridge.

It was called to the Bar at Middle Temple Inn in London. 

22

Manchar Lake receives water mainly from which river?

Correct Answer: Indus
Show explanation
Explanation

Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Jamshoro Sindh Pakistan.

It collects water from the Kithar mountain range.

It is one of South Asia's largest.

Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Pakistan.

Manchhar Lake covers an area of  23.5 km (14.6 mi).

It is part of Indus River basin.

23

Female Inspector of which province named Irum Khanam won two gold medals in world powerlifting Championship 2025?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


Additional Information:

The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

***

24

Name the Pakistani police female inspector who won gold medal in world powerlifting Championship 2025?

Correct Answer: Irum Khanum
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


Additional Information:

The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

***

25

Where has World’s largest markhor sculpture been unveiled?

Correct Answer: Kaghan Valley
Show explanation
Explanation

In December 2025, Kaghan Valley became home to the world’s largest sculpture of the Markhor, Pakistan’s national animal, installed near the Shah Dawood Palace site in the Batakundi area.

The massive structure took five years to complete.

Height: 105 feet high and 38 feet wide

****

26

Who has become the first ISI chief in Pakistan to be court martialed?

Correct Answer: Lieutenant General (r) Faiz Hameed
Show explanation
Explanation

On 11 Dec 2025, Former chief of Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) Faiz Hameed was sentenced to 14 years imprisonment by a military court.

He was sentenced on charges including violation of state secrets and interfering in politics.

Note: It is the first time that an ISI chief in Pakistan has been court martialled.

 

Additional info:

Faiz Hameed remained chief of ISI from 2019 to 2021.

 ***

27

River Ravi rises in the South East of Pir Punjal range and joins the River Chenab. Its length is ____ kms.

Correct Answer: 765
Show explanation
Explanation

The Ravi River is a transboundary river crossing northwestern India and eastern Pakistan.

Ravi River length: 765 km 

28

Khalafat movement was abolished in which year?

Correct Answer: 1924
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khilafat movement start in 1919-

The Khilafat movement ended in 1924

Khalafat movement started in India during world war 1 

For the purposes to protect the khalafat

The Ottoman Caliphate,  was abolished on 3 March 1924  by decree of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey.

The process was one of Atatürk's Reforms following the replacement of the Ottoman Empire with the Republic of Turkey.


29

The famous Hiran Minar is located at _____?

Correct Answer: Sheikhupura
Show explanation
Explanation

Hiran Minar is located in Sheikhupura


It was built during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Jahangir.


Its construction began in 1607.


Its construction was completed in 1620.

30

Derawar Fort is located in ______?

Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
Show explanation
Explanation

The height of walls of Derawar Fort is 40 meters.

It is located in Cholistan Desert, Ahmadpur East Tehsil, Bahawalpur.

The walls have a perimeter of 1500 metres and stand up to 30 metres high.

31

MPA stands for _________?

Correct Answer: Member of Provincial Assembly
Show explanation
Explanation

A Member of the Provincial Assembly or MPA, is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district to the legislature or legislative assembly of a subnational jurisdiction.

32

How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

Indus River: The principal river, flowing through the entire province, vital for irrigation and life. 

Malir River: Flows through Karachi, often considered more of a drain now. 

Lyari River: Another Karachi river, also largely a drain. 

Hub River: Forms part of the Sindh-Balochistan border. 

33

Height of Islamabad from sea level is _________?

Correct Answer: 1700 feet
Show explanation
Explanation
Islamabad is located on the northern edge of the Pothohar Plateau
At the foot of the Margalla Hills
Its elevation is 540 meters (1,770 ft)

Additional information:
Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.
It is the country's ninth-most populous city
With a population of over 1.2 million people
It was Constructed in 1960
34

Fatima Jinnah ________ of Quaiz e Azam

Correct Answer: younger sister
Show explanation
Explanation

Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

Fatima Jinnah born: 30th July 1893

Fatima Jinnah is referred as Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

Fatima Jinnah died: on 9 July 1967

Fatima Jinnah died at the age: of 71

Fatima Jinnah died: Karachi

Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

Question-related to Fatima Jinnah

In Pakistan, the second Presidential Elections were held on 2 January 1965.


Fatima Jinnah was a presidential candidate against Ayub Khan


There were four candidates: Ayub Khan, Fatima Jinnah and two obscure people with no party affiliation. 


Laltain was the election symbol of Fatima Jinnah in the Presidential Election in 1965


Nominee
Ayub Khan
Fatima Jinnah
Party
CML
COP

49,951

35

Quaid-e-Azam was sworn in as the first Governor General of Pakistan on?

Correct Answer: 15 August 1947
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The oath of Governor-General administered to Quiad-e-Azam by Justice Abdul Rasheed
  • ***
  • Sir Mian Abdul Rashid,was the first Chief Justice of Pakistan.
  • On 15 August 1947, when Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was sworn in as the first Governor-General of Pakistan, Rashid, as the most senior Muslim judge in British India, administered the oath of office to him.

1st Governor-General of Pakistan 

Quaid e Azam born on: 25 December 1876 in Karachi

Died on: 11 September 1948

In-office: 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan

Succeeded by Khawaja Nazimuddin

36

Baran river is flowing in the province of?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

The Baran river, also known as the Nai Baran river or Baran Nai,

It is situated in the Sindh province of Pakistan

A hill torrent that ranges from the Sindh to the Balochistan provinces of Pakistan, at the foot of the Kirthar Mountains range.

37

Masjid Wazir Khan was made in which century?

Correct Answer: 17th
Show explanation
Explanation

The Wazir Khan Mosque is a 17th-century mosque located in the city of Lahore.

The mosque was commissioned during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

Opened: 1642

***


38

Which train was attacked by Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) in March 2025 killing more than 20 passengers?

Correct Answer: Jaffar Express
Show explanation
Explanation

The train named Jaffar Express hijacked by BLA in Balochistan in March 2025.

The train was targeted by terrorists in the area of Bolan, Balochistan on March 11, 2025.

The banned Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) claimed responsibility for the attack.

 

Additional Information:

As per ISPR, all 33 terrorists killed by Security forces.

21 passengers and 4 Frontier Corps personnel dead in this incident

***
39

1965 war started from ______?

Correct Answer: 6 September
Show explanation
Explanation

The 1965 war between India and Pakistan began on April 6, 1965 with the start of Operation Gibraltar.

India retaliated by crossing the international border at Lahore on September 6, 1965 and attacking Lahore and Kasur.

The war lasted 17 days.

The war ended with a ceasefire declared on September 22, 1965 after diplomatic intervention by the Soviet Union and the United States.

40

Which party has most seats in Senate?

Correct Answer: Pakistan Peoples Party
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) currently holds the most seats in the Pakistani Senate

Number of Seats: 26 out of 96 total seats.


Political groups

*Government (71)

  •   PPP (26)
  •   PMLN (19)
  •   JUI (F) (6)
  •   BAP (4)
  •   MQM-P (4)
  •   ANP (3)
  •   NP (1)
  •   PML(Q) (1)
  •   IND (6)

*Opposition (15)

  •   MWM (1)
  •   IND (15)
41

Rakaposhi is also known as Dumani, which means?

Correct Answer: Mother of Mist
Show explanation
Explanation

Rakaposhi is also known as Dumani, meaning "Mother of Mist" or "Mother of Clouds."

Additional information 


Rakaposhi is the 27th-highest mountain in the world.

It is the steepest peak in the world.

It is located in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan.

Rakaposhi rises over the Nagar Valley.

It is the part of the Karakoram range.

It is the 12th highest mountain in Pakistan.

The first successful ascent was in 1958 by Mike Banks and Tom Patey.

42

The 'Bloodless Revolution' or 'Glorious Revolution' took place in which year?

Correct Answer: 1688
Show explanation
Explanation

The Glorious Revolution (or Bloodless Revolution) took place in 1688 in England

Key Figures: James II (deposed), William of Orange (William III), and Mary II (his wife).

Significance: Established that the monarch's power was limited by Parliament, laying the groundwork for modern democracy. 

43

Indian National Congress formed in 1885. Who was the Viceroy at that time?

Correct Answer: Lord Dufferin
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Viceroy of India at the time the Indian National Congress was formed in 1885 was Lord Dufferin.
  • The Indian National Congress was formed when 72 representatives from all over the country met at Bombay in 1885.
  • The Indian National Congress dominated the Indian movement for independence from Great Britain.
44

In 1942, Muslim League did not take part in _____?

Correct Answer: Quit India Movement
Show explanation
Explanation

The Muslim League did not support the Quit India Movement because it was aimed at both Muslims and then British. Instead, the League demanded "Divide and Quit".

The Muslim League, the Communist Party of India, and the Hindu Mahasabha did not support the movement.

45

FATA was merged with KPK under which constitutional amendment?

Correct Answer: 25th
Show explanation
Explanation

FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK,

FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats.

The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

*********

46

The Election of Chief Minister shall be held by:

Correct Answer: Open Ballot
Show explanation
Explanation

The election of the Chief Minister shall be held by the members of the legislative assembly or any other specified body,

depending on the electoral process outlined in the constitution or relevant legislation.

47

As per Article 10A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, for the determination of his _______ obligations or in any criminal charge against him a person shall be entitled to a fair and due process.

Correct Answer: Civil rights
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 10A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 ensures the right to fair trial and due process.

It applies in the determination of a person’s civil rights and obligations or in any criminal charge against him.

48

According to the 1956 Constitution, who could be the head of state of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: A Muslim only
Show explanation
Explanation

According to the 1956 Constitution of Pakistan, only a Muslim could be elected as the President (Head of State).

This was the first constitution of Pakistan and it officially declared Pakistan as an Islamic Republic.

49

Article 25 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with?

Correct Answer: Equality of citizens
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 25 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with Equality of citizens.

Article 25(A) of constitution of 1973 deals with Right to education.

50

Any right which has been provided by the Constitution of 1973 is called —— ?

Correct Answer: Fundamental right
Show explanation
Explanation

Any right specifically provided for and guaranteed by the Constitution of 1973 is called a Fundamental right.

These rights are considered essential for the comprehensive development of citizens and are legally enforceable through the high courts and Supreme Court of Pakistan. 

51

The parliament of Pakistan consists of______?

Correct Answer: National assembly, senate and president
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Upper House of Parliament of Pakistan is Senate
  • ****
  • The Parliament of Pakistan is bicameral.
  • Article 50 of the Constitution states that the Parliament of Pakistan consists of the President and Prime Minister
  • two Houses are known as
    •             the National Assembly (lower house)
    •             the Senate. (upper house)
  • ****

  • agm.04.03.23


52

Which constitutional amendment restored parliamentary democracy after Gen Zia's Marshal law?

Correct Answer: 8th
Show explanation
Explanation

The Eighteenth Amendment to Pakistan's constitution 

 Passed in 2010

 It restored parliamentary democracy by reducing presidential powers.

It reversed many changes made during military rule, especially under General Zia-ul-Haq.

53

Which Pakistani University was ranked among the top 100 in Asia in 2025?

Correct Answer: Quad-i-Azam
Show explanation
Explanation

In the QS World University Rankings: Asia 2025 were ranked in the top 100

National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST)  at 67th 

Quaid-i-Azam University (QAU) at 84th



54

First English Music channel of Radio Pakistan is based in _____?

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

On 14 November, PBC launched its first English Music Channel in Islamabad called Planet 94.

The network operates on FM 94.

55

Which of the following Cash Crop of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Cotton
Show explanation
Explanation

The five major crops in Pakistan are:

  1. Cotton
  2. Wheat
  3. Rice
  4. Sugarcane
  5. Maize

Cotton is the most important cash crop.

It followed by rice, which is the second main staple food crop and a major export commodity.

Cash crop means نقدی فصل

56

Which city is called ‘Brasilia of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamabad is called the Brazil of Pakistan

  • because Islamabad is the capital of Pakistan
  • and Islamabad is a very neat and clean city

It has been the capital of Pakistan since 1967.

It is the country's ninth-most populous city,



57

The Quit India Movement started at:

Correct Answer: Bombay on Aug 8, 1942
Show explanation
Explanation

Know everything about Quit India Movement

Started: Aug 8, 1942

Started from: Bombay 

Launched by: Gandhi

Also known as: August Movement 

Demand: to end British rule

Jinnah changed the slogan "Divide and Quit India" 

Started by party: Congress

Quaid e Azam said about the Quit India movement an anti-Muslim action of congress

ND23-4-2023

58

Rawal Dam is built on the river _____?

Correct Answer: Korang
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Rawal Dam was built in 1962 by the Punjab Government.
  • It supplies water to Rawalpindi and Islamabad.
  • The dam is constructed on the Korang River.
  • The Korang River originates in the Murree Hills.
  • It flows toward Islamabad.
    • 59

    The biggest library in Pakistan is?

    Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam Library
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Quaid-e-Azam Library is a public library located within the Bagh-e-Jinnah in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

    The library was constructed in the mid-19th century during the British Raj compromises of Victorian era Lawrence and Montgomery Halls. 

    Established: 1886

    The complex includes two halls, the first was built in memory of John Laird Mair Lawrence, 1st Baron Lawrence,

    and the second in memory of Robert Montgomery Martin

    60

    The first Prime Minister of Pakistan was:

    Correct Answer: Liaqat Ali Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    List of PM of Pakistan
    S/No Name Tenure
    1 Mr. Liaqat Ali Khan 15-08-1947 To 16-10-1951
    2 Khawaja Nazimuddin 17-10-1951 To 17-04-1953
    3 Mr. Mohammad Ali Bogra 17-04-1953 To 11-08-1955
    4 Ch. Mohammed Ali 11-08-1955 To 12-09-1956
    5 Mr. Hussain Shaheed Suharwardi 12-09-1956 To 18-10-1957
    6 Mr. Ibrahim Ismail Chaundrigar 18-10-1957 To 16-12-1957
    7 Malik Feroz Khan Noon 16-12-1957 To 07-10-1958
    8 Mr. Noor-ul-Amin 07-12-1971 To 20-12-1971
    9 Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto 14-08-1973 To 05-07-1977
    10 Mr. Muhammad Khan Junejo 23-03-1985 To 29-05-1988
    11 Mohtrama Benazir Bhutto 02-12-1988 To 06-08-1990
    Mr. Ghulam Mustafa Khan Jatoi (Caretaker) 06-08-1990 To 06-11-1990
    12 Mian Mohammed Nawaz Sharif 06-11-1990 To 18-04-1993
    Mir Balakh Sher Mazari (Caretaker) 18-04-1993 To 26-05-1993
    Mian Mohammed Nawaz Sharif 26-05-1993 To 08-07-1993
    Mr. Moin Qureshi (Caretaker) 08-07-1993 To 19-10-1993
    13 Mohtrama Benazir Bhutto 19-10-1993 To 05-11-1996
    Malik Meraj Khalid (Caretaker) 06-11-1996 To 17-02-1997
    14 Mian Mohammed Nawaz Sharif 17-02-1997 To 12-10-1999
    15 Mir Zafarullah Khan Jamali 23-11-2002 To 26-06-2004
    16 Chuadhary Shujaat Hussain 30-06-2004 To 26-08-2004

    Jamrud Fort is located near ________ ?

    Correct Answer: Peshawar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    at the entrance to the Khyber Pass from the Peshawar side in the tribal districts of KPK, Pakistan

    Jamrud Fort Opened: 1836

    62

    What is the total area of Sindh Province?

    Correct Answer: 140,914 Sq Km
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

    Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by land area

    Sindh is the second-largest province by population after Punjab

    Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam (the gateway of Islam),


    Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 7 administrative divisions

    The mountain range located in Sindh is the Kirthar Range

    There are three Barrages in Sindh

    The mountain range located in Sindh is the Kirthar Range

    Sindhi is written in a version of the Arabic script.

    Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

    The Sindhi language is the oldest language

    Karachi is the biggest Division of Sindh by population and Hyderabad is the largest Division of Sindh by area.

    What is the total estimated forest area of Sindh ?

    0.92 million hectares

    In this conference, 161 Muslim delegates from all parts of India decided to create opportunities for Muslims to get higher education.

    Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner, Professor Dr. Abdul Salam, got his Master's in Mathematics from _____?

    Correct Answer: Government College, Lahore
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Professor Dr. Abdul Salam, Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner, received his Master's degree in Mathematics from Government College, Lahore in 1946.

    He later went on to earn his PhD in Physics from Cambridge University.

    He was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 for his work on the electroweak unification of the fundamental forces of nature.

    When did Simon commission arrive in India?

    Correct Answer: 3rd February 1928
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ·        The Indian Statutory Commission also known as Simon Commission,

    ·        was a group of seven Members of Parliament

    ·        under the chairmanship of Sir John Simon.

    ·        The commission arrived in India in 1928

    ·        to study constitutional reform in Britain's largest and most important possession.

    Simon Commission wanted to check the working of Government of India Act, 1919.

    80

    Gomal Pass is located near the city of:

    Correct Answer: Zhob
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Gomal Pass is located near the city of zhob valley

    Gomal Pass is a mountain pass on the Durand Line border between Afghanistan and the southeastern portion of South Waziristan in Pakistan's KPK 

    It takes its name from the Gomal River and is midway between the legendary Khyber Pass and the Bolan Pass.

    It connects Ghazni in Afghanistan with Tank and Dera Ismail Khan in Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: 1983

    Kenya

    Tanzania

    Uganda

    the United Kingdom

    Afghanistan.

    KANUPP is located near ?

    Correct Answer: Baleji Beach
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    KANUPP stands for Karachi Nuclear Power Plant

    it is a large commercial nuclear power plant located at Paradise Point in Karachi Sindh 

    it is Pakistan's first nuclear energy plant

    it is composed of three commercial nuclear power plants

    K1: 1 August 1966 (support from Canada) 

    K2: 20 August 2015 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

    K3: 31 May 2016 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

    Show explanation

    It is a governmental organization responsible for environmental protection and conservation in Pakistan.

    Headquarters (HQ): Islamabad, Pakistan

    Founded in: 1997 (under the Pakistan Environmental Protection Act, 1997)

    PASSCO stands for?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Agriculture Seed Supplies Corporation
    Show explanation

    PATA stands for_____?

    Correct Answer: Provincially Administered Tribal Areas
    Show explanation

    It was the former administrative subdivision of Pakistan designated in Article 246(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan.

    86

    Pakistan’s Standard time was adopted on _________?

    Correct Answer: September 1951
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Pakistan had been following UTC+05:30 since 1907
    • continued using it after independence in 1947
    • ***

    • Pakistan's standard time was suggested by Professor Muhammad Anwar in 1951
    • **
    • On 15 September 1951, Mathematician Mahmood Anwar, two time zones were introduced.
    • Pakistan's standard time was adopted on 15 September 1951.

    Who was the founder of a college known as Madrassah-e-Rahimyah?

    Correct Answer: Shah Abdur Rahim
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The founder of Madrassah-e-Rahimyah was Shah Abdur Rahim.

    **More Information's***

    It was founded by Shah Abdur Rahim, the father of Shah Waliullah Dehlawi,

    During the reign of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb.

    After the death of Shah Abdur Rahim in 1718 Shah Waliullah started teaching at the Madrasah.

    ****

    Established: 18th century

    Affiliation: Naqshbandi Sufism, Hanafi school

    Location: Delhi, India

    Who founded Urdu Defence Association?

    Correct Answer: Mohsin-ul-Mulk
    Show explanation

    The Urdu Defence Association was started in April 1900


    The Urdu Defence Association started the advocacy for Urdu as the lingua franca of the Muslim community of India. 



    The association is regarded as an offshoot of the Aligarh Movement.

    Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk, Munir Nawaz Jang, also known as Syed Mehdi Ali


    Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk was an Indian Muslim politician.


    Nawab Mohsin-﻿ul-Mulkwas involved in the Aligarh Movement


    Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk was one of the founders of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

    What is Father name of Allama Iqbal?

    He was Died: 21 April 1938

    His Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

    93

    How many census have done in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 7
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are the Total 7 Census held in Pakistan:

    1. 1951: The first census after Pakistan's independence.
    2. 1961: The second census.
    3. 1972: The third census, delayed due to the 1971 war.
    4. 1981: The fourth census.
    5. 1998: The fifth census.
    6. 2017: The sixth census.
    7. 2023: The 7th population and housing census was conducted digitally.
    94

    What was Mother tongue of Quaid-e-Azam?

    Correct Answer: Gujrati
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's mother tongue was Gujarati.

    His family migrated to Karachi from the Kathiawar region of Gujarat, and they spoke Gujarati at home. 

    95

    Which is the Largest Division in Pakistan in terms of area?

    Correct Answer: Kalat Division
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Qalat is the largest division with regard to area.

    Lahore is the largest division of Pakistan with regard to population.

    96

    Dr. Abdus Salam, the Nobel Prize winner of Pakistan, belonged to ______?

    Correct Answer: Punjab
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhang Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

    In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

    Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

    He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

    Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

    In 1952 he Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge.

    97

    Which was the first public airline of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Orient airline
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Orient airline was the first and only Muslim-owned airline.

    Orient airline was established on 29 October 1946.

    Orient Airlines was initially based in Calcutta, India.

    Orient airline shifted to the independent state of Pakistan in 1947.

    Orient airline was rechristened as Pakistan International Airlines in 1955

    Orient airline is the old name of PIA airlines.

    98

    CSP stands for?

    Correct Answer: Central Superior Services
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Central Superior Services (CSS)

    It is responsible for running the bureaucratic operations and government secretariats and directorates of the Cabinet of Pakistan.

    Formation: 26 September 1973

    99

    Makli Hill Graveyard is located in which city?

    Correct Answer: Thatta
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Makli Necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981.

    Makli Necropolis, located in Thatta, Pakistan.

    It is one of the largest funerary sites in the world.

    It is the final resting place of some 125,000 local rulers, Sufi saints, and other eminent persons from the 14th to the 18th century.

    100

    The next senior rank to Sub Inspector in Police is SHO/Inspector. What does SHO stand for?

    Correct Answer: Station House Officer
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    SHO stands for Station House Officer. 

     The SHO holds the rank of inspector or sub-inspector.

    The rule of SHO in Pakistan is the overall supervision of the functions of each police station

    SHO is the head or officer in charge of a police station of a locality.

    101

    What was the distance between East and West Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1600 miles
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The distance between East and West Pakistan was approximately 1600 miles.

    India lay in between, separating the two wings.
    102

    What is the height of Minar e Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 70 m
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Height: 70 m

    Architect: Nasreddin Murat-Khan

    Construction started: March 23, 1960

    Location: Lahore, Punjab; Pakistan

    Opened: October 21, 1968

    Architectural styles: Modern architecture, Mughal architecture


    103

    Gomal Zam Dam is in which province?

    Correct Answer: KPK
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Gomal Zam Dam is a gravity dam in South Waziristan Tribal District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

    The dam impounds the Gomal River.

    104

    Khewara mines are famous for:

    Correct Answer: Sodium Chloride deposits
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Khewra Salt Mine is in Khewra, north of Pind Dadan Khan, in Jhelum District, Punjab Region, Pakistan.

    The mine is in the Salt Range, Potohar plateau, which rises from the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

    It is the second largest in the world

    105

    What is the length of the silk highway route in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 887 KM
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

    The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

    The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

    The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

    The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

     It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

    Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

    Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

    Karakorum highway is also called N-35

    ****

    ND9-11-2022

    Abbreviation of NIAB

    Correct Answer: Nuclear Institute of Agriculture and Biology
    Show explanation

    NIAB stands for Nuclear Institute of Agriculture and Biology

    It is located in Faisalabad, Punjab.

    Founded: April 6, 1972

    109

    Shahnama-i-Islam was written by _____?

    Correct Answer: Hafeez Jalandhari
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shahnama-i-Islam was written by Hafeez Jalandhari.

    He is also famous for writing the lyrics of Pakistan’s national anthem.

    110

    Bala Hisar Fort is located in _______?

    Correct Answer: Peshawar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Babur first built a fort here in 1526 after capturing Peshawar.

    First mentioned by 7th-century explorer Xuanzang, the fort was used as a royal residence for the Durrani Empire since December 1747.

    The Maratha Empire captured it after the Battle of Peshawar in 1758.

    The Sikhs reconstructed the fort after capturing Peshawar in March 1823.

    111

    The first invasion of Mahmud Ghaznvi was in ______?

    Correct Answer: 1000 AD
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The first invasion of Mahmoon Ghaznavi against India was in 1000 AD. 

    Mahmood Ghazni is known as the “Abductor of Scholars”.

    17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in 1027 AD.

    112

    Who made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan______?

    Correct Answer: Ayoub Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.

    Ayoub Khan made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan

    The area of Islamabad before its declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known as Raj Shahi.

    Islamabad is the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million people

    Constructed 1960

    Postcode 44000

    ND23-4-2023

    113

    Who is the first lady fighter pilot of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Ayesha Farooq
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ayesha Farooq is the first lady fighter pilot of Pakistan.

    She achieved this milestone in 2013.

    She becoming a symbol of women's empowerment in the Pakistan Air Force.

    114

    The first shaheed female pilot of Pakistan Air Force is ______?

    Correct Answer: Marium Mukhtiar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Flying Officer Marium Mukhtiar, born on May 18, 1992.

    She was a Pakistani fighter pilot who died on November 24, 2015, when her PAF FT-7PG aircraft crashed near Kundian, Mianwali.

    She was the first female combat pilot of Pakistan to die in a crash on duty.

    115

    Who was the first female commercial pilot of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Shukria Khanum
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shukriya Khanum (c. 1935 – 14 May 2017) was a Pakistani pilot.

    She was Pakistan’s first woman to obtain a commercial pilot license.

    She was died of cancer in 2017 at the age of 82 in Lahore.

    116

    How many rivers are there in Balochistan____?

    Correct Answer: 7
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

    The important rivers in Balochistan are:

    Zhob

    Nari

    Bolan

    Pishin

    Lora

    Mula

    Hub

    Porali

    Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

    Rakshan

    Dasht.

    ***

    while

    24 rivers are present in Pakistan

    5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

    4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

    7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

    8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

    ND16-3-2023





    117

    Which city is called the Gateway of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Karachi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Karachi is called the "Gateway of Pakistan"

    It has an ideal location and well-developed connections to form gateways region with:

    • Afghanistan,
    • Central Asia,
    • and Western China



    Additional information:

    It was the first capital of Pakistan.

    It is the biggest city in Pakistan.

    The capital of Karachi is Sindh.


    118

    Who was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1965?

    Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are three major Wars (overall 04) fought between India and Pakistan from 1947 to 2021

     

    1. The first conflict over the status of the state of Jammu and Kashmir was in 1947.

    ·         It is also called as first Kashmir war.

    ·         Result: As a result of the 1947 war, Pakistan gained control of about onr-thirds of the Kashmir region.

     

     

     

    2. The second conflict between Pakistan and India over the status of the state of Jammu and Kashmir was in 1965.

    ·         Pakistan's Operation Gibraltar is the main reason for the 1965 war.

    ·         Ayub Khan was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1965.

    ·         Lal Bahadur Shastra was the prime minister of India during the Indo­ Pak war 1965.

     

     

     

    3. The third conflict between Pakistan and India was in 1971.

    ·         Yahya Khan was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1971.

    ·         Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister of India during the Indo-Pak war in 1971.

     

     

    4. There is also a limited war in 1999 at Kargil between India and Pakistan.

     

    119

    Dr Abdul Salam was born in which city of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Jhang
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhang Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

    In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

    Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

    He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

    Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

    In 1952 he Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge.

    120

    After the creation of Pakistan, first newspaper was?

    Correct Answer: Imroze
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The first Urdu language newspaper published in the newly independent Pakistan was the Daily Imroze,

    They were founded by Mian Iftikharuddin in 1947,

    Originally published in Lahore. 


    121

    After how many years Pakistan got its first constitution?

    Correct Answer: 9 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    After 09 years Pakistan got its first constitution.

    on 23rd March 1956 first constitution of Pakistan was enforced.

    Choudhry Mohammad Ali was the Prime Minister of Pakistan during the enforcement of the first constitution.

    There were 234 articles 13 parts and 6 schedules.

    After the abrogation of the first constitution, the new constitution was enforced by Ayoub Khan on June 1962

    AGM 02.05.2023



    122

    East Pakistan was separated from West Pakistan on _______?

    Correct Answer: 16 December 1971
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 16 December 1971, East Pakistan was liberated from Pakistan as the newly independent state of Bangladesh.

    East Pakistan was the eastern provincial wing of Pakistan between 1947 and 1971.

    Its land borders were with India and Burma, with a coastline on the Bay of Bengal.

    India firstly recognized to Bangladesh.

    The first currency note of SBP was issued on___________?

    Correct Answer: March 1, 1949
    Show explanation

    Pakistan’s first coin was issued on 3rd January 1948



    124

    Karachi remained capital of Pakistan from:

    Correct Answer: 1947 to 1959
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Karachi remained capital of Pakistan from 1947 to 1959.

    Rawalpindi remained capital of Pakistan from 1959 to 1967.

    Islamabad is the current capital of Pakistan since 14 August 1967.

    Name the architect who designed the Shah Faisal Masjid, located in Islamabad?

    Correct Answer: Vedat Dalokay
    Show explanation

    The Faisal Mosque is the largest in Pakistan,

    located in the national capital city of Islamabad.

    Completed in 1986,

    Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay designed it

    Faisal Mosque of Pakistan was opened to the public in 1986 OR 1987

    **

    On 18th June 1988 the first prayer  was held in the Faisal mosque

    What was the old name of PIA?

    Correct Answer: Orient Airways
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Orient Airways was the old name of PIA

    Orient Airways Ltd. was an airline established in 1946 new name Pakistan International Airlines in 1955

    The airline shifted operations to the newly independent state of Pakistan in 1947.

    Pakistan International Airlines is an international airline of Pakistan

    Headquarters: Karachi

    Founded: October 29, 1946

    Founder: Mirza Ahmad Ispahani

    PIA flight landed in China in 1963

     PIA, in 1964 became the first non-Communist airline to fly to China

    127

    What is the second highest peak in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Naga Parbat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The second-highest peak in Pakistan is Nanga Parbat,

    Which stands at 8,126 meters (26,660) feet

    It is the ninth-highest mountain in the world and is located in the Himalayas.  

    The meaning of Nanga Parbat is Naked Mountain

    It is also called Killer Mountain Peak.

    Location Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan

    Nanga Parbat, known locally as Diamer

    The mountain range is Nanga Parbat is in the Himalayas, Western Himalaya


     

    128

    What is the other name of Nanga parbat ?

    Correct Answer: Diamir
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    Nanga Parbat, known locally as Diamer

    Diamir is a local name for the peak and means “king of the mountains.”


    The height of Nanga Parbat at 8,126 m (26,660 ft) above sea level.

    Worldwide Nanga Parbat is the 9th highest mountain.


    The meaning of Nanga Parbat is Naked Mountain


    It is also called Killer Mountain Peak.

     

    In 1954, the mountain peak of Nanda Parbat was conquered

     

    First ascent July 3, 1953

     

    Location Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan

     

    Mountain range is Nanga Parbat is Himalayas, Western Himalaya

    129

    Daspang is another name of which mountain?

    Correct Answer: K-2
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dapsang is another name for K2

    Dapsang is the name used by locals in the Balti language. 


    K2 was given the initial "K" designation because it was the second mountain surveyed in the Karakoram Range

    K2: The original survey name

    Chogori: Another local name for K2, which means "king of mountains".

    Mount Godwin Austen: Named after Henry Godwin-Austen, an English geographer who surveyed the mountain.


    130

    Which mountain range is located in Norther areas of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Hamaliya
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Northern Pakistan is dominated by three major mountain ranges:
    • The Karakoram
    • The Himalayas
    • The Hindu Kush.
    These ranges converge in this region, which is home to many of the world's highest peaks, including K2. 
    Karakoram:
    Located in the northernmost parts of Pakistan, it contains the second-highest peak in the world, K2. 
    Himalayas:
    This range runs along the western side of Pakistan and includes the peak Nanga Parbat, the highest in the country. 
    Hindu Kush:
    Situated in the northwest of Pakistan, this range extends into Afghanistan and has the peak Tirich Mir as its highest point in the region. 
    131

    Which city of Pakistan is called shaheeno ka shehar?

    Correct Answer: Sargodha
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shaheenon Ka Shahar means (A city of Eagles)

    Sargodha is also known as the City of Eagles

    Sargodha is famous for its orange.

    Sargodha is Pakistan's 12th-largest city by population

    Sargodha was founded by the British in 1903

    What form of Government is in place in Pakistan presently?

    Correct Answer: Parliamentary democracy
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan currently operates under a parliamentary democracy, where the Prime Minister is the head of government, and the President holds a largely ceremonial role.

    This system allows elected representatives to govern and make decisions on behalf of the citizens.

    142

    Pakistan's first and largest Islamic bank is :

    Correct Answer: Meezan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Meezan Bank is Pakistan's first and largest Islamic bank

    In 2000, Meezan Bank was listed on the Karachi Stock Exchange.


    Founded: 27 January 1997 by Noor Financial, Pak Kuwait Investment Company, and Islamic Development Bank.

    Headquarters: Karachi


    143

    What us the largest and oldest commercial bank in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: HBL
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Habib Bank Limited (HBL) is identified as the largest and oldest commercial bank in Pakistan

    Habib Bank Limited is a Pakistani multinational bank.

    HBL was founded in 1940 by the Habib family.

    HBL became Pakistan's first commercial bank.

    In 1951 it opened its first international branch in Colombo, Sri Lanka.

    The Government nationalized the bank in 1974 and privatized it in 2003.

    In 2003 the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development acquired a controlling share.

    HBL major shareholding lies with the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) which is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

     

    As of 2018, HBL has 1700+ branches with presence in over 25 countries spanning four continents.

    144

    According to SBP Act 2021, the governor of State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) shall be appointed for a term of________?

    Correct Answer: Five Years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to SBP Act 2021, the governor of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) shall be appointed for a term of five Years

    ***

    Mr. Jameel Ahmad assumed the charge of Governor State Bank of Pakistan on August 26, 2022, after his appointment by the President of Pakistan for a period of 5 years.

    Ms. Kamil is the first woman appointed as Deputy Governor of SBP.

    Sh1-9-2023


    145

    At present Pakistan has vast natural resources and items of mineral as many as________________?

    Correct Answer: 52
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mineral resources can be divided into two major categories - Metallic and Nonmetallic.

    Metallic resources are things like Gold, Silver, Tin, Copper, Lead, Zinc, Iron, Nickel, Chromium, and Aluminum. Nonmetallic resources are things like sand, gravel, gypsum, halite, Uranium, dimension stone

    146

    The largest size of iron resources in Pakistan is located at ________?

    Correct Answer: Kalabagh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Iron ore is found in various regions of Pakistan including Nokundi, Chiniot, Kalabagh the largest one, Haripur, and other northern areas.

    The iron ore resources at Kalabagh are 400 million metric tons 

    while Nokkundi reserves are 50 million tons and Dilband iron ore deposits

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Mercantile Exchange
    Show explanation

    It is the country's first futures commodity market and is regulated by the Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan (SECP).

    State Bank of Pakistan is also known as?

    Correct Answer: Central Bank of Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is the central bank of Pakistan.

    It is also known as the SBP. 


    Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the SBP on July 1, 1948. 

    Its headquarters are in Karachi, Pakistan's financial capital. 

    The SBP's operations are based on the State Bank of Pakistan Act of 1956. 

    153

    State Bank of Pakistan is run by which of the following ? Board of managers, Board of governors, Board of directors, None of these

    Correct Answer: Board of directors
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is governed by a Board of Directors, which oversees the bank’s affairs under the SBP Act.

    154

    Who was UN General Assembly President in 1962-1963?

    Correct Answer: Sir Zuffarullah Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sir Muhammad Zafarullah Khan of Pakistan was the President of the United Nations General Assembly from 1962 to 1963.

    He was Pakistan's first foreign minister and led the case for Pakistan's separate nation in the Radcliffe Commission.

    He also served as a judge on the International Court of Justice from 1954 to 1958, and as its vice president from 1958 to 1961.

    In 1970, he became the first and only Pakistani to serve as the President of the International Court of Justice.

    155

    The Pothohar Plateau contains the mineral deposits?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

    the total area of about 5000 miles2

    located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River

    300-600 meters is the height of the Pothohar Plateau
    ******

    The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

    Jhelum

    Chakwal

    Rawalpindi

    Attock

    ****

    The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

    Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

    District Rawalpindi District
    Islamabad Capital Territory
    Chakwal District
    Jhelum District
    Khushab District,
    Mirpur District
    Islamabad
    Mianwali District,
    Sargodha District (Northern Belt)
    ND13-5-2023

    156

    Pakistan most important and major crop is ___

    Correct Answer: Cotton
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The most important crops are wheat, sugarcane, cotton, and rice, which together account for more than 75% of the value of total crop output. 

    Cotton is the most important cash crop in Pakistan and cotton products export account for 55 percent of all foreign exchange earnings of the country.

    ------

    Major Kharif crops are:

    1.       Rice 

    2.       Sugarcane

    3.       Cotton

    4.       Maze

    5.       Bajra

    6.       Jawar

    When the period of Kharif crop starts and when ends?

    Strat: April-June,      

    End:  Sept - Oct

     

    When the period of Rabi crop starts and when ends?

    These crops are planted in winter from October to December

    harvested in summer from March to May

     

    Which are Pakistan major seasons for agriculture crop?

    Kharif & Rabi

    157

    The most important Cash Crop of Pakistan after Wheat is:

    Correct Answer: Rice
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are five major crops namely:

    1. Cotton
    2. wheat
    3. rice
    4. sugar cane
    5. maize

    Cotton is the most important cash crop in Pakistan in terms of area and value addition.

    After Wheat, Rice is the second main staple food crop

    Rice second major exportable commodity after cotton.


    ***

    nd12-2022

    Which Muslim leader was declared Man of the Year by the Muslim 500?

    Correct Answer: Imran Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation
