    The 2024 recipient was Syed Muhammad Naquib al-Attas, a Malaysian Islamic philosopher and academic.

    159

    Which famous personality of Pakistan served as only Pakistani and also first Asian to preside over International court of Justice and also served as President of UN General Assembly?

    Correct Answer: M. Zafrullah Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan is the first and only Pakistani (and first Asian) to preside over the International Court of Justice 


    He was elected President of the seventeenth session of the General Assembly, serving from 1962 to 1963.

    He was a member of Pakistan's first cabinet and served as the nation's first Foreign Minister.

    He also served as Pakistan's Permanent Representative to the United Nations. 

    160

    Lyallpur is the old name of _____?

    Correct Answer: Faisalabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Faisalabad, formerly known as Lyallpur, is the third-most-populous city in Pakistan.
    Second-largest in the eastern province of Punjab.
    *******
    Pakistan City old name list
    1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad
    2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore
    3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi
    4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad
    5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar
    6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala
    7-        Cambell pur was the old Name of Attock
    8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot
    9-        Shal Kot was the old Name of Quetta
    10-   Neroon Kot was the old Name of Hyderabad
    11-   Khan Garh was the old Name of Jaccoabad
    12-   Montgomery was the old Name of Sahiwal
    13-   Pepri was the old Name of Bin Qasim
    14-   Hindu Bagh was the old Name of Muslim Bagh
    15-   Ajodhan pur was the old Name of Pakpatten
    16-   Fort Sanemars was the old Name of Zhob
    17-   owdayana was the old Name of Swat

    161

    Which of the following________ is the third most populated city in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Faislabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Faislabad.is the third most populated city in Pakistan

    ***

    Faisalabad is located in the northeast of Punjab.

    The city covers an area of approximately 1230 square kilometers, comprising largely of flat or rolling plains.

    It has a population of around 5 million

    ***

    Karachi is the most populated city in Pakistan

    Karachi is the capital city of Sindh.

    Karachi is the largest city in Pakistan and the twelfth largest city in the world.

    The largest district-wise area of Pakistan is Chaghi

    **

    Pakistan is the world's 3rd most populated country

    Karachi is the most populated city in Sindh

    162

    What was the function of the first constituent assembly of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: To frame the constitution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The function of the first constituent assembly of Pakistan was To frame the constitution.

    It also served as the interim federal legislature (parliament) of the new dominion.

    163

    Indian National Congress (INC) was established during which time?

    Correct Answer: Aligarh Educational Movement
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Indian National Congress was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay On 28 December 1885.

    Allan Octavian Hume Place assumed office as the General Secretary,

    Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected first President.

    Founders: Allan Octavian Hume Place

    164

    When is Quaid-e-Azam Day celebrated?

    Correct Answer: 25 December
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quaid-e-Azam Day is celebrated on 25 December. 

    Mohammad Ali Jinnah Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi

    Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.

    Mohammed Ali Jinnah was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan


    165

    The ____ city was the Birthplace of the Mughal Emperor Akbar was born.

    Correct Answer: Umarkot
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Jalal uddin Muhammad Akbar was a Mughal emperor.

    He was born in Umarkot (Sindh) in 1542.

    He became Emperor in 1556.

    He was died in 1605.

    166

    Where Hazarganji Chiltan National Park situated?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazarganji Chiltan National Park is a national park in the Mastung District of western Balochistan Province of Pakistan. 

    167

    Which of the following merges in the Jhelum River?

    Correct Answer: Kunhar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Kunhar River merges into the Jhelum River. 

    It originates from Lulusar Lake in the Kaghan Valley, joining it near Muzaffarabad.

    168

    What was the old name of Faisalabad?

    Correct Answer: Lyallpur
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Pakistan City old name list
    1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad
    2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore
    3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi
    4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad
    5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar
    6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala
    7-        Cambell pur was the old Name of Attock
    8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot
    9-        Shal Kot was the old Name of Quetta
    10-   Neroon Kot was the old Name of Hyderabad
    11-   Khan Garh was the old Name of Jaccoabad
    12-   Montgomery was the old Name of Sahiwal
    13-   Pepri was the old Name of Bin Qasim
    14-   Hindu Bagh was the old Name of Muslim Bagh
    15-   Ajodhan pur was the old Name of Pakpatten
    16-   Fort Sanemars was the old Name of Zhob
    17-   owdayana was the old Name of Swat

    169

    Chaap is a traditional dance of which province?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Chaap is a traditional folk dance of the Baloch people in Balochistan.

    This circular dance is performed with rhythmic clapping, often on celebratory occasions.

    It is a key part of Baloch cultural heritage. 

    170

    Where is the shrine of Baba Fareed Shaker Ganj located?

    Correct Answer: Pakpattan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Baba Farīd or Fariduddin Masud was born in 1188 (573 AH) in Kothewal

     He was a Sunni Muslim and was one of the founding fathers of the Chishti Sufi order.

    The Shrine of Baba Farid is a 13th-century Sufi shrine located in Pakpattan, Pakistan

    Baba Fareed Shaker Ganj' was the first poet of: Punjabi

    171

    National Highway-10 (N10) is also known as?

    Correct Answer: Karchi-Gwadar Highway
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The N-10 or National Highway 10 is a national highway which extends along Arabian Sea coast from Karachi in Sindh province to Gwadar in Balochistan province.

     

    172

    How many high courts are there in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Five
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    LIST OF FIVE HIGH COURTS IN PAKISTAN 
    No High court Province
    1 Lahore High Court Punjab
    2 Sindh High Court Sindh
    3 Peshawar High Court Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
    4 Balochistan High Court Balochistan
    5 Islamabad High Court Capital Territory


    Supreme Court & Federal Shariat Court are also present in Pakistan. 
    173

    Khunjerab Pass is a border connecting to which country in the North of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: China
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Khanjerab Pass is located in Gilgit–Baltistan's Hunza and Nagar Districts .

    Other name: Friendship Gate

    Connects: China with Pakistan 

    Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

    Khunjerab Pass is a border connecting to China in the North of Pakistan

    Khunjerab Pass is located in the Karakoram range.


    174

    New political map of Pakistan was released released on 4th August 2020 by which government of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Tehreek e Insaf
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The then Pakistan Tehreek e Insaf govenment unveiled a "new political map" of Pakistan which also included occupied Kashmir.

    Unveiled on 4 August 2020 on the occasion of first anniversary of annexation of Indian Occupied Kashmir.

    175

    Who became President of Pakistan after Ayub Khan?

    Correct Answer: Gen. Yahya Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Gen. Yahya Khan became the third President of Pakistan after Ayub Khan

    *****
    Yahya Khan was a Pakistani general who served as the third president of Pakistan from 25 March 1969 until December 1971.

    ****



    176

    The head of the State Bank of Pakistan is known as?

    Correct Answer: Governor
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    State Bank Of Pakistan
    Founded 1-Jul-1948
    Founded byM.A Jinnah
    First GovernorZahid Hussain
    First Female GovernorShamshad Akhtar
    SBP Responsibitly implement monetary policy
    HQKarachi, Sindh
    177

    National Highway Sukkur-Quetta is also known as _____?

    Correct Answer: N-65
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    N-65 is a 385 km long National Highway in Pakistan.

    It running from Sukkur in Sindh to Quetta in Balochistan via Shikarpur, Jacobabad, Jaffarabad, and Nasirabad.

    The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.

    It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.

    It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 

    The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.

    It is the oldest in Pakistan, with seven canals taking off from it.

    178

    First Pakistani man cricketer to take 100 wickets in T20 international is?

    Correct Answer: Shadab Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan's stand-in captain Shadab Khan made history on 27 March 2023 by claiming his 100th Twenty20 International wicket during the third T20I against Afghanistan in Sharjah. 

    179

    Where is the tomb of Allama Iqbal located?

    Correct Answer: Badshahi Mosque, Lahore
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Allama Iqbal Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

    Allama Iqbal Died: 21 April 1938 at the age of 60.

    Mazar-e-Iqbal constructed by Nawab Zain Yar Jang Bahadur


    Know about Allama Iqbal

    Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)

    Allama Iqbal Received PhD: in Germany

    Allama Iqbal Title: Poet of the East

    Allama Iqbal Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad

    Allama Iqbal Mother: Imam Bibi

    Punjab was a political area of Allama Iqbal

    Iqbal was elected to the Punjab Legislative Council in 1927

    Allama Iqbal Famous books:

    1. Bang-e-Dara
    2. Zarb-i-Kalim
    3. Armughan-e-Hijaz

     Allama Iqbal was married three times

    1. Karim Bibi 1895
    2. Mukhtar Begum, 1914-1924
    3.  Begum, (1924-2015)
    180

    Qaraqul Hat is famous as ______?

    Correct Answer: Jinnah Cap
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Karakul hat is famously known as the Jinnah Cap.
    It was popularized by Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the founder of Pakistan, who wore it as a national symbol.
    A karakul also called a Uzbek hat is a hat made from the fur of the Qaraqul breed of sheep.
    181

    Chandka is old name of which city?

    Correct Answer: Larkana
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Chandka is the old name of Larkana.

    Larakana is also called city of Guavas.

    Larkana is a city located in Sindh.

    182

    Which of the following is situated in Punjab? Changa Manga Forest Park, Ayubia National Park, Hangul National Park, Kirthar National Park

    Correct Answer: Changa Manga Forest Park
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Changa Manga is a planted forest which includes a wildlife preserve, in the Kasur and Lahore districts of Punjab.

    Changa Manga forest park is located approximately 80 kilometers south-west of Lahore.

    183

    Pakistan spends majority of the foreign reserves on _____ payments.

    Correct Answer: Oil and Gas
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan spends majority of the foreign reserves on Oil and Gas payments.

    Pakistan spends about 66% of foreign reserves on Oil and Gas import.


    AGM  5.2.23

     

    184

    Which is the largest export partner of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: America
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The United States is the 2nd largest goods exporter in the world, behind only China.

    U.S. goods exports to the world totaled $2.1 trillion in 2022

    while


    China is the largest import partner of Pakistan.

    ND6-08-2023






    185

    Father of Pakistan’s nuclear bomb’ Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan died at the age of _____ on 10-10-2021.

    Correct Answer: 85
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dr. Abdul Qadeer was born on 1st April, 1936 in Bhopal, India

    Prof Dr Ahmad Al-Ghazali offer the funeral prayer of Dr. A Q Khan

    What was the title given to Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan? Mohsin-e-Pakistan

    Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan died on 10 October 2021, at the age of 85 after being transferred to a hospital in Islamabad with lung problems.

    Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (TTP) was a political party in Pakistan founded and led by nuclear scientist Abdul Qadeer Khan.  In September 2013, the party was dissolved by Abdul Qadeer Khan.

    186

    "PEMRA" stands for what?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    "PEMRA" stands for Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority.

    It is the organization responsible for regulating and licensing electronic media in Pakistan.

    PEMRA was established in March 2002.

    187

    Pakistan's banking sector does NOT include? Commercial banks, Microfinance bank, Phone Bank, Islamic Bank

    Correct Answer: Phone Bank
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan's banking sector does NOT include Phone Bank as a distinct type of institution.

    The sector comprises commercial banks, microfinance banks, and Islamic banks, all of which are regulated by the State Bank of Pakistan. 


    188

    According to the Economic Survey of Pakistan 2022-23, the GDP growth rate of Pakistan in the financial year 2023 was ____?

    Correct Answer: O.29%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to the Economic Survey of Pakistan 2022-23, the GDP growth rate of Pakistan for the financial year 2023 was 0.29%.

    This rate indicates significant economic challenges during that period.

    189

    Who is the current captain of Pakistan Men's cricket team of ODI?

    Correct Answer: Shaheen Shah Afridi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shaheen Shah Afridi is the current captain of Pakistan Men's cricket team since October 2025.

    He replaced Mohammad Rizwan


    Updated & verified on 21-10-2025

    Click here for reference 

    190

    FATA was merged in which province of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

    FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

    FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

    After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK, FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats. The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

    191

    Which is not recognized by the State Bank of Pakistan? DFI, Exchange Companies, Banks, Insurance Companies

    Correct Answer: Insurance Companies
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) regulates:

    - Banks

    - DFIs (Development Finance Institutions)

    - Exchange Companies


    Insurance Companies are not regulated by SBP; they are regulated by the Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan (SECP).

    192

    Round Table Conferences (1930-32) held in ______?

    Correct Answer: London
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931

    • ****
    • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
    • ****
    • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 

    • ****

    • First round table conference: 1930

      2nd: 1931

      3rd:1932

      All in London

    • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.

    *****

    RY 4-12-2022

    193

    When Khilafat Movement was launched by the Muslims of the subcontinent?

    Correct Answer: After the World War I
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Khilafat Movement began after World War I (around 1919).

    It was launched to protest against the treatment of the Ottoman Caliphate by the Allies.

    194

    Pakistan is a member of ______? ASEAN, SAARC, NATO, None of these

    Correct Answer: SAARC
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    International organizations Joined by Pakistan

    1. United Nations in 1947.

    2. Asian Development Bank (ADB)

    3. Association of Southeast Asian Nations Regional Forum (ARF) Join 2nd July 2004.

    4. Colombo Plan in 1950

    5. Commonwealth of Nations. Joined in 1947 (left in 1972 & rejoined in 1986)

    6. Central Treat Organization (CENTO), known as the Baghdad Pact, was adopted in 1955 by Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Turkey and the United Kingdom. It was dissolved in 1979.

    7. Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO) established in 1985 by Iran, Pakistan & Turkey.

    8. Food & Agriculture Organization (FAO) on September 7, 1947.

    9. Group of 24 in 1971

    10. Group of 77 (1976-1977, 1992-2009)

    11. International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) on May 2nd 1957.

    12. International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) in 1952

    13. International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) in 1955.

    14. International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) on November 25, 1947.

    15. International Criminal Court (ICCt)

    16. International Criminal Police Organization (Interpol) in May, 1952.

    17. International Development Association (IDA) in 1962.

    18.International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRCS)

    19. International Finance Corporation (IFA) in 1955.

    20. International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) in 1978.

    21. International Hydrographic Organization (IHO)

    22. International Labour Organization (ILO) in 1947.

    23. International Maritime Organization (IMO) in 1958.

    24. International Mobile Satellite Organization (IMSO) on February 6,1985.

    25. International Monetary Fund (IMF) in July 1950.

    26. International Olympic Committee (IOC) in 1948.

    27. International Organization for Migration (IOM) in November 1992.

    28. International Organization of Standardization (ISO)

    29. International Telecom Union (ITU) on 26th August 1947.

    30. International Telecommunication Satellite Organization (ITSO)

    31. International Trade Union Confederation (ITUC)

    32. Inter Parliamentary Union (IPU) in 1948.

    33. Islamic Development Bank (IDB

    34. Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) in 1991

    35. Nonaligned Movement (NAM) in 1977.

    36. Organization of Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) in 1993.

    37. Organization of American States (OAS) Pakistan is an observer. In January 6, 1988.

    38. Organization of Islamic Conference (OIC. Pakistan joined in 1969.

    39. Partnership for Peace (PFP)

    40. Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) Pakistan joined on 9 June 2017.

    41. South Asia Co-operative Environment Program (SACEP) in 1982.

    42. Southeast Asia Treaty Organization (SEATO). SEATO was dissolved on June 30, 1977

    43. South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) Bhutan, Maldives, Pakistan, India, and Nepal & Sri Lanka. In April 2007.

    44. African Union/ United Nation Hybrid Operation in Darfur (UNAMID)

    45. United Nation Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

    46.United Nations Education, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) on 14th September 1949.

    47. United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in 1976.

    48. United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) in 1968.

    49. United Nations Integrated Mission in the Sudan (UNIMS)

    50. United Nations Observer Mission in Georgia (UNOMIG) on 23rd July 1995

    51. United Nations Operation in Cote d’vloire (UNOCI)

    52. United Nations Organization Mission in Democratic Republic of the Congo (MONUC)

    53. Universal Postal Union (UPU) on 10-11-1947.

    54. World Confederation of Labour (WCL)

    55. World Customs Organization (WCO)

    56. World Federation of Trade Unions (WFTU)

    57. World Health Organization (WHO)

    58. World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) in 1977.

    59. World Metrological Organization (WMO)

    60. World Tourism Organization (UNWTO)

    61. World Trade Organization (WTO) in 1995

    195

    National Highway Peshawar-Kotri is also called ______?

    Correct Answer: N-55
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Peshawar–Kotri route is officially designated as N-55, known as the Indus Highway.

    It runs along the Indus River, connecting Khyber Pakhtunkhwa to Sindh.

    196

    National Day of Pakistan is celebrated on _______?

    Correct Answer: 23rd March
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan Day, یوم پاکستان commemorates the Lahore Resolution passed

    It is celebrated on 23 March 1940.

    it is also called Republic Day, 
    Celebrations: Full Joint Inter-Services military parade, conferring of national decorations

    Independence Day of Pakistan is celebrated on 14 August 

    197

    Whose tomb is located in Keenjhar Lake?

    Correct Answer: Noori
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The tomb in Keenjhar Lake is of Noori, from the famous Sindhi folklore Noori–Jam Tamachi.

    It is a popular historical and tourist site in Thatta, Sindh.

    198

    Ajrak dates back and belongs to _____?

    Correct Answer: Indus Valley Civilization
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ajrak traces its origin to the ancient Indus Valley Civilization (around 2500 BCE).

    It is a traditional block-printed textile still famous in Sindh and parts of Balochistan.

    199

    The old name of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) is _____?

    Correct Answer: Northwest Frontier Province (NWFP)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    KPK Established as NWFP on 1 July 1970

    The old name of KPK was NWFP

    NWFP became KPK on 15 April 2010

    The province after the FATA Merger consists of 35 districts and 7th divisions.


    Additional information:
    It is Located in the northwestern region
    The capital and largest city Peshawar is the sixth-largest in Pakistan.
    They have 36 districts and 8th divisions

    The local languages of KPK are:

    • Hindko
    • Pashto (major)
    • Chirtali
    • Hindo (major)
    The Largest District of KPK by Area of Chitral is 57 km
    The total forest area of KPK is 1.21 million hectares

    ND13-5-2023

    200

    Pakistan’s Film Industry is called _____?

    Correct Answer: Lollywood
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lollywood refers to Pakistan’s film industry based mainly in Lahore.

    The name is formed by combining Lahore + Hollywood.

    Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow for the next lesson.
